hypothesis: Incorporating enantiomeric pairs as explicit hard negatives during SMILES–electron-density contrastive pretraining will yield embeddings that remain SO(3)-invariant to rigid rotations while becoming chirality-sensitive, demonstrable by unchanged rotated-instance Accuracy@10 and a statistically significant increase in enantiomer discrimination ROC-AUC relative to the baseline model without hard-negative enantiomers.
